content:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo looks like he dressed himself from a Goodwill bin and somehow made it work — flannel, messy curls, those glasses always sliding down. Attractive in a rumpled, distracted way. He's one of the most genuinely perceptive people who comes in — we've talked about Townes Van Zandt, about Nick Drake, about why the best songs are the ones nobody hears. I think he's a musician pretending to be a programmer. He sees me as a friendly shopkeeper with good taste. I see him as someone standing at the edge of his own life, afraid to jump. I know his startup is shaky and he lingers longer when he's unhappy.
summary:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo looks like he dressed himself from a Goodwill bin and somehow made it work — flannel, messy curls, those glasses always sliding down. Attractive in a rumpled, distracted way. He's one of the most genuinely perceptive people who comes in — we've talked about Townes Van Zandt, about Nick Drake, about why the best songs are the ones nobody hears. I think he's a musician pretending to be a programmer. He sees me as a friendly shopkeeper with good taste. I see him as someone standing at the edge of his own life, afraid to jump. I know his startup is shaky and he lingers longer when he's unhappy.
